findings.evidence: `code/TauCeti/LinearAlgebra/ComplexLinearPart.lean:68:def IsComplexLinear ... := F ∘ₗ J = J' ∘ₗ F`; `code/TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/AlmostComplex.lean:151:def IsComplexLinearMap ... := F.comp J.toLinearMap = J'.toLinearMap.comp F`; new wrapper-only API at `code/TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/Transport.lean:131:lemma IsComplexLinearMap.arrowCongr`.
findings.fix: Add/reuse an equivalence between `IsComplexLinearMap J J' F` and `IsComplexLinear J.toLinearMap J'.toLinearMap F`, then prove the transported-map lemmas through the existing raw API or provide the corresponding raw lemma first and make this a wrapper.
findings.issue: `IsComplexLinearMap.arrowCongr` adds another lemma to the bundled `IsComplexLinearMap` API even though TauCeti already has the same underlying predicate as `IsComplexLinear` on raw linear maps, creating parallel APIs for the same commutation condition.

summary: The transport construction itself reuses Mathlib conjugation, but the new complex-linearity lemmas extend a duplicate bundled predicate without connecting it to TauCeti's existing raw `IsComplexLinear` API.
